title: Automata Forge
description: A from-scratch theory-of-computation lab spanning the WHOLE Chomsky hierarchy. NEWEST — v6 Parse mode, a complete from-scratch PARSER GENERATOR built on the FIRST/FOLLOW engine: the top-down LL(1) predictive table M[A,a] (with conflict detection for left-recursion / common-prefix grammars) and the ENTIRE bottom-up tower LR(0) < SLR(1) < LALR(1) < LR(1) — the canonical item-set automaton (closure/goto, LR(1) lookahead propagation via FIRST(βa), and LALR by core-merging proven to yield exactly |LR(0)| states), drawn with the app's pan/zoom/export renderer; the ACTION/GOTO parse tables with shift-reduce & reduce-reduce conflicts flagged; a live grammar CLASSIFIER (which classes the grammar belongs to, and why the rest fail); and ANIMATED stack-driven parsers — a predictive LL(1) parser building the leftmost tree and a shift-reduce LR parser building the rightmost tree in reverse, both step-scrubbable. A curated gallery puts each grammar on a different rung (Knuth's pointer grammar is LALR(1)-not-SLR(1); the classic reduce/reduce-on-merge grammar is LR(1)-not-LALR(1); dangling-else is an unavoidable shift/reduce). Every parser is differential-tested to accept exactly L(G) against the Earley oracle. REGULAR (level 3): compile a regex into a parse tree, Thompson e-NFA, subset-construction DFA, Hopcroft-minimized DFA and a Brzozowski-derivative DFA; step strings through any of them; compare two regexes via the product automaton (union, intersection, difference, symmetric difference), decide equivalence/containment and prove inequality with the shortest distinguishing string; or draw your own automaton in Build mode, with a Myhill-Nerode table and direct NFA-to-regex. CONTEXT-FREE (level 2) — Grammar mode: analyze a grammar (nullable / generating / reachable / useless, FIRST & FOLLOW), normalize to Chomsky Normal Form step-by-step, recognize strings two ways (CYK table + Earley chart), render the derivation tree with ambiguity detection, sample the language, compile to a pushdown automaton with an animated stack, and play the CFL pumping lemma. NEW in v5 — Machine mode, the TOP of the hierarchy (level 0/1): a single-tape Turing machine simulator with an animated two-way tape, full configuration trace, a state-transition diagram and a delta-table, a linear-bounded (context-sensitive) mode, and a gallery of deciders for languages no grammar can describe — a-n b-n c-n and w#w are NOT context-free yet a TM decides them live — plus transducers, Rado's busy beaver, and a nondeterministic-TM configuration search. Bridges run both ways: every regex becomes a right-linear grammar AND a read-only Turing machine. No libraries — every parser, automaton algorithm, the graph layout and the SVG renderer are hand-written and differential-tested.
